page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton2 processor, utilizing the 64-bit ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz with one thread per core, eliminating simultaneous multithreading. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

This instance type is optimized for networking, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates strong memory bandwidth performance, with read-only large block transfers reaching 181.11 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25% of tested servers. However, single-core capabilities show some limitations; the single-threaded gzip compression rate of 20.34 GB/sec and the Geekbench single-core score of 973.0 are positioned in the bottom 25%. Multi-core performance remains balanced, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48563.42 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11689.0, both falling within the average range.

The c6gn.8xlarge offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that benefit from high network throughput and dedicated ARM cores without requiring local NVMe storage. The 2.0 GB of RAM per core ratio makes it suitable for compute-heavy tasks rather than memory-intensive databases. Based on its performance profile, this instance is suited for network-intensive applications, static web serving, multi-threaded data processing, and distributed compute workloads that can leverage ARM64 architecture.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.8xlarge is a compute-optimized, network-optimized instance powered by a 32-core AWS Graviton2 ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but offers a high baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong memory bandwidth for large blocks at 181.11 GB/sec, though single-core performance metrics, such as a Geekbench single-core score of 973.0, are relatively weak. Multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48563.42. This server is suited for network-intensive workloads, static web serving, and multi-threaded ARM64 applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 64 GB RAM, 50 Gbps network bandwidth, and strong large-block memory read speeds.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.8xlarge is a 32-core ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 64 GB RAM and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated cores, 50 Gbps network bandwidth, and strong large-block memory read performance.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput networking
Strong large-block memory read bandwidth of 181.11 GB/sec
Suited for static web serving, multi-threaded processing, and ARM64 workloads
